source_jobs.title: Mechanical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Reflex Robotics is building affordable ($10k) wheeled humanoid robots to automate dangerous and repetitive tasks in manufacturing and logistics.

We envision a future where intelligent robots are doing all kinds of boring work that people hate doing—loading chicken nuggets into Costco boxes, lifting forty pound bags of dog food at Petco stores, and cleaning up cranberry juice spills in your apartment.

We are a three-year-old startup backed by Khosla Ventures, with $60M/year of revenue lined up pending successful pilots with e-commerce warehouses in 2025.




[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/a269dfb1-ec2d-4a9f-a1c9-d7977516a5bc/48549dee-55c4-4d8e-acbb-405cf9feac1c/CopyofReflexStrengthDemoRiceBag-ezgif.com-optimize.gif]


How Does It Work?

Our robots are designed and built entirely in-house by an engineering team that led development of the Stretch robot at Boston Dynamics and key systems on the Tesla Model S, X, and Y production lines. Reflex robots are high-performance, low-inertia, and optimized for low-cost manufacturing.

We’ve built the best real-time teleoperation system in the world, allowing a remote operator in South America to “play a video game” to control our robots at human-level speeds. This has allowed us to already ship robots with positive unit economics, and enables us to create a powerful human-intervention + RL product feedback loop.

Our system allows us to collect high-quality demonstrations at scale—giving us the proprietary data engine needed to train increasingly capable AI systems. We're on track to build the largest robotics dataset in the world, which will serve as an important long-term advantage.


Key Company Beliefs

 - High-quality, proprietary robotics data is the next foundation for generational AI companies (like Tesla FSD and ChatGPT).

 - Being nerd-sniped by maximizing an engineering metric is way less important than solving our customers’ biggest pain points.

 - An insane work ethic is required for outsized success—and you'll be rewarded for it.


What We’re Looking For

We are looking for experienced Mechanical Engineers to join our team.

We’re still a small team—which means high ownership, high equity, and the chance to shape the product from the ground up.

You should strongly consider applying if:

 - You like to understand the entire system you’re working with and making multidisciplinary trade offs – e.g. actuators, power electronics, communication protocols, controls approaches, structures, etc.

 - You have worked with multi-DOF system (e.g., robot arms, 3D printers, rocket gimbal actuators)

 - You have hands-on experience building things and are able to write clear documentation to manage technicians

 - You have shipped production hardware and pride yourself on making robust assemblies that last for years

We use Solidworks for CAD.

You’d be joining a company that already has a solid core business—with working hardware, delighted customers, and profitable unit economics. Reflex is de-risked enough to see the hazy outlines of success, but still small enough that there’s enormous upside up for grabs.

Compensation: $80,000–$250,000, depending on experience, skills, and qualifications


Come Join Us

This is a rare opportunity to help build a flagship robotics company from the ground up—and to do work that will truly matter, reshaping what people believe is possible in robotics.

We love to see the things you’ve worked on. Have a portfolio or insane project you’ve worked on? Share it. We’re looking for people who push past the status quo, are passionate at work and in their own time—we’re looking for people who want to win.

source_jobs.title: Electrical Engineer (Hiring at all Levels)
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Reflex Robotics is building affordable ($10k) wheeled humanoid robots to automate dangerous and repetitive tasks in manufacturing and logistics.

We envision a future where intelligent robots are doing all kinds of boring work that people hate doing-loading chicken nuggets into Costco boxes, lifting forty pound bags of dog food at Petco stores, and cleaning up cranberry juice spills in your apartment.

We are a three-year-old startup backed by Khosla Ventures, with $100M+/year of revenue lined up pending successful pilots with e-commerce warehouses.




[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/a269dfb1-ec2d-4a9f-a1c9-d7977516a5bc/48549dee-55c4-4d8e-acbb-405cf9feac1c/CopyofReflexStrengthDemoRiceBag-ezgif.com-optimize.gif]


How Does It Work?

Our robots are designed and built entirely in-house by an engineering team that include alumni from Boston Dynamics, Tesla, Waymo, Amazon Robotics, Intel, Figure, ASML and more. Reflex robots are high-performance, low-inertia, and optimized for low-cost manufacturing.

We’ve built the best real-time teleoperation system in the world, allowing a remote operator in South America to “play a video game” to control our robots at human-level speeds. This has allowed us to already ship robots with positive unit economics, and enables us to create a powerful human-intervention + RL product feedback loop.

Our system allows us to collect high-quality demonstrations at scale, giving us the proprietary data engine needed to train increasingly capable AI systems. We’re on track to build the largest robotics dataset in the world, which will serve as an important long-term advantage.


Key Company Beliefs

High-quality, proprietary robotics data is the next foundation for generational AI companies (like Tesla FSD and ChatGPT).

Being nerd-sniped by maximizing an engineering metric is way less important than solving our customers’ biggest pain points.


What We’re Looking For

An Electrical Engineer to take design, layout, testing, and deployment of electronics on the Reflex robot. This is a high-impact role on a small team moving quickly with a lot of autonomy.

Someone who can operate in a small team which means high ownership, high equity, and the chance to shape the product from the ground up.

Someone who has built something they’re proud of. Whether it's a side project, an open-source contribution, or a product feature that shipped to real users.




Required Qualifications

 - BS or higher in Electrical Engineering or related field

 - Generalist across electronics domains: motor inverter design, power electronics, sensors, analog circuits, communications (Ethernet, CAN, SPI, I2C, GMSL, MIPI-CSI, RS-485), and MCUs

 - Proficiency in Altium for schematic capture and PCB layout

 - Strong board bring-up, test, and debug skills: comfortable with a scope, a power supply, and a problem

 - Experience taking designs from prototype through production: DFM, supply chain considerations, and test coverage

 - Ability to work independently in ambiguity, extracting requirements and making design decisions without waiting for direction

 - Understanding of system-level tradeoffs: how your board interacts with firmware, mechanical constraints like cable routing and connectors, and cost


Bonus Qualifications

 - Firmware development (C/C++, RTOS)

 - High-speed digital design (DDR4, PCIe, MIPI)

 - Motherboard design with integrated CPU/GPU

 - Robotics experience

 - RF design

 - Simulation tools (LTspice, PSpice)
   

Compensation: $140,000–$250,000, depending on experience, skills, and qualifications

Come Join Us

Reflex is de-risked enough to see the hazy outlines of success, but still small enough that there’s enormous upside for grabs. This is a rare opportunity to help build a flagship robotics company from the ground up and to do work that will truly matter, reshaping what people believe is possible in robotics.

source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Reflex Robotics is building affordable ($10k) wheeled humanoid robots to automate dangerous and repetitive tasks in manufacturing and logistics.

We envision a future where intelligent robots are doing all kinds of boring work that people hate doing—loading chicken nuggets into Costco boxes, lifting forty pound bags of dog food at Petco stores, and cleaning up cranberry juice spills in your apartment.

We are a three-year-old startup backed by Khosla Ventures, with $60M/year of revenue lined up pending successful pilots with e-commerce warehouses in 2025.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/a269dfb1-ec2d-4a9f-a1c9-d7977516a5bc/48549dee-55c4-4d8e-acbb-405cf9feac1c/CopyofReflexStrengthDemoRiceBag-ezgif.com-optimize.gif]


How Does It Work?

Our robots are designed and built entirely in-house by an engineering team that led development of the Stretch robot at Boston Dynamics and key systems on the Tesla Model S, X, and Y production lines. Reflex robots are high-performance, low-inertia, and optimized for low-cost manufacturing.

We’ve built the best real-time teleoperation system in the world, allowing a remote operator in South America to “play a video game” to control our robots at human-level speeds. This has allowed us to already ship robots with positive unit economics, and enables us to create a powerful human-intervention + RL product feedback loop.

Our system allows us to collect high-quality demonstrations at scale—giving us the proprietary data engine needed to train increasingly capable AI systems. We're on track to build the largest robotics dataset in the world, which will serve as an important long-term advantage.


Key Company Beliefs

 - High-quality, proprietary robotics data is the next foundation for generational AI companies (like Tesla FSD and ChatGPT).

 - Being nerd-sniped by maximizing an engineering metric is way less important than solving our customers’ biggest pain points.

 - An insane work ethic is required for outsized success—and you'll be rewarded for it.


What We’re Looking For

We are looking for controls engineers to join our team!

We’re still a small team—which means high ownership, high equity, and the chance to shape the product from the ground up.

You should strongly consider applying if:

 - You’ve controlled the motion of real-world hardware before, not just in simulation—e.g., nanometer-scale control of EUV lithography machines, or making a quadruped do a backflip

 - You like to understand the entire system you’re working with—e.g., you care about details like time-sync between two processors, or the stiffness properties of the mechanical assembly

 - You have a good understanding of model predictive control, nonlinear optimization, sensor fusion strategies

 - You pride yourself on writing robust code that others can trust for years

We primarily use C++, with some Python for prototyping. We don’t use ROS.

You’d be joining a company that already has a solid core business—with working hardware, delighted customers, and profitable unit economics. Reflex is de-risked enough to see the hazy outlines of success, but still small enough that there’s enormous upside up for grabs.

Compensation: $215,000–$300,000, depending on experience, skills, and qualifications

Come Join Us

This is a rare opportunity to help build a flagship robotics company from the ground up—and to do work that will truly matter, reshaping what people believe is possible in robotics.

We love to see the things you’ve worked on. Have a portfolio or insane project you’ve worked on? Share it. We’re looking for people who push past the status quo, are passionate at work and in their own time—we’re looking for people who want to win.

We are looking for an Electrical and Network Technician to join our team.

What You Will Do

 - System Debugging - You are the first line of defense for robot electrical issues

 - SMT Rework, Oscilloscope, Cable Harness Production, PCB Design

 - Network testing, setup, debug at HQ and customer sites

 - Mechanical Assembly

 - Inventory Management and Quality Control

 - Resolve issues with Engineering team

 - Create detailed written & verbal team updates

 - Occasionally travel to customer sites (Driver's license strongly preferred)


Required Skills

 - A minimum of 3+ years of experience working with complex electromechanical or robotic systems, including diagnostics and troubleshooting.

 - Very comfortable with a terminal

 - Multidisciplinary knowledge spanning controls engineering, mechanical dynamics, systems integration, test development, data processing, and firmware analysis.

 - Extensive experience with hand tools, power tools, and common shop tools

 - Must be a self-starter, organized, and reliable

 - Efficient working independently and in collaborative environments.

 - Carefully read, follow, and update assembly instructions

 - Authorized to work in USA (Ideally already lives near Brooklyn)

You’d be joining a company that already has a solid core business—with working hardware, delighted customers, and profitable unit economics. Reflex is de-risked enough to see the hazy outlines of success, but still small enough that there’s enormous upside up for grabs.

source_jobs.title: Principal AI Research Engineer - World Models
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Reflex Robotics is building affordable ($10k) wheeled humanoid robots to automate dangerous and repetitive tasks in manufacturing and logistics.

We envision a future where intelligent robots are doing all kinds of boring work that people hate doing—loading chicken nuggets into Costco boxes, lifting forty pound bags of dog food at Petco stores, and cleaning up cranberry juice spills in your apartment.

We are a three-year-old startup backed by Khosla Ventures, with $60M/year of revenue lined up pending successful pilots with e-commerce warehouses in 2025.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/a269dfb1-ec2d-4a9f-a1c9-d7977516a5bc/48549dee-55c4-4d8e-acbb-405cf9feac1c/CopyofReflexStrengthDemoRiceBag-ezgif.com-optimize.gif]


How Does It Work?

Our robots are designed and built entirely in-house by an engineering team that led development of the Stretch robot at Boston Dynamics and key systems on the Tesla Model S, X, and Y production lines. Reflex robots are high-performance, low-inertia, and optimized for low-cost manufacturing.

We’ve built the best real-time teleoperation system in the world, allowing a remote operator in South America to “play a video game” to control our robots at human-level speeds. This has allowed us to already ship robots with positive unit economics, and enables us to create a powerful human-intervention + RL product feedback loop.

Our system allows us to collect high-quality demonstrations at scale—giving us the proprietary data engine needed to train increasingly capable AI systems. We're on track to build the largest robotics dataset in the world, which will serve as an important long-term advantage.


Key Company Beliefs

 - High-quality, proprietary robotics data is the next foundation for generational AI companies (like Tesla FSD and ChatGPT).

 - Being nerd-sniped by maximizing an engineering metric is way less important than solving our customers’ biggest pain points.

 - An insane work ethic is required for outsized success—and you'll be rewarded for it.


What We’re Looking For

We’re still a small team—which means high ownership, high equity, and the chance to shape the product from the ground up.

VLAs and other great “base policies” for robotics achieve ~80% success rates, but in real robot deployments, it’s essential to achieve 99.99% success rates. We can’t ask our customers to tolerate our robots packing three socks into a bin instead of four, or swapping shipping labels between two packages—not even once!

You should apply for this role if:

 - You’ve architected and trained large-scale video/world models (ex. Sora, Veo)

 - You’ve turned generative models into controllable simulators: action‑/state‑conditioned rollouts

 - You’ve built the data & eval stack for world modeling to quantify downstream policy success

You’d be joining a company that already has a solid core business—with working hardware, delighted customers, and profitable unit economics. Reflex is de-risked enough to see the hazy outlines of success, but still small enough that there’s enormous upside up for grabs.


Come Join Us

This is a rare opportunity to help build a flagship robotics company from the ground up—and to do work that will truly matter, reshaping what people believe is possible in robotics.

We love to see the things you’ve worked on. Have a portfolio or insane project you’ve worked on? Share it. We’re looking for people who push past the status quo, are passionate at work and in their own time—we’re looking for people who want to win.

source_jobs.title: Principal AI Research Engineer - RL
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Reflex Robotics is building affordable ($10k) wheeled humanoid robots to automate dangerous and repetitive tasks in manufacturing and logistics.

We envision a future where intelligent robots are doing all kinds of boring work that people hate doing—loading chicken nuggets into Costco boxes, lifting forty pound bags of dog food at Petco stores, and cleaning up cranberry juice spills in your apartment.

We are a three-year-old startup backed by Khosla Ventures, with $60M/year of revenue lined up pending successful pilots with e-commerce warehouses in 2025.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/a269dfb1-ec2d-4a9f-a1c9-d7977516a5bc/48549dee-55c4-4d8e-acbb-405cf9feac1c/CopyofReflexStrengthDemoRiceBag-ezgif.com-optimize.gif]


How Does It Work?

Our robots are designed and built entirely in-house by an engineering team that led development of the Stretch robot at Boston Dynamics and key systems on the Tesla Model S, X, and Y production lines. Reflex robots are high-performance, low-inertia, and optimized for low-cost manufacturing.

We’ve built the best real-time teleoperation system in the world, allowing a remote operator in South America to “play a video game” to control our robots at human-level speeds. This has allowed us to already ship robots with positive unit economics, and enables us to create a powerful human-intervention + RL product feedback loop.

Our system allows us to collect high-quality demonstrations at scale—giving us the proprietary data engine needed to train increasingly capable AI systems. We're on track to build the largest robotics dataset in the world, which will serve as an important long-term advantage.


Key Company Beliefs

 - High-quality, proprietary robotics data is the next foundation for generational AI companies (like Tesla FSD and ChatGPT).

 - Being nerd-sniped by maximizing an engineering metric is way less important than solving our customers’ biggest pain points.

 - An insane work ethic is required for outsized success—and you'll be rewarded for it.


What We’re Looking For

We’re looking for stellar on-policy RL engineers to work on creating robust robot policies.

We’re still a small team—which means high ownership, high equity, and the chance to shape the product from the ground up.

VLAs and other great “base policies” for robotics achieve ~80% success rates, but in real robot deployments, it’s essential to achieve 99.99% success rates. We can’t ask our customers to tolerate our robots packing three socks into a bin instead of four, or swapping shipping labels between two packages—not even once!

You should apply for this role if:

 - You’ve re-implemented core RL algorithms (SAC, DDPG) from scratch and can debug unstable gradients / tune hyperparameters correctly

 - You’ve made meaningful intellectual contributions to sample-efficient RL algorithms (e.g., DreamerV3 and MuZero)

 - You’ve shipped on-policy RL on hardware that learns in the real-world (e.g., for quadruped walking or drone racing)

You’d be joining a company that already has a solid core business—with working hardware, delighted customers, and profitable unit economics. Reflex is de-risked enough to see the hazy outlines of success, but still small enough that there’s enormous upside up for grabs.


Come Join Us

This is a rare opportunity to help build a flagship robotics company from the ground up—and to do work that will truly matter, reshaping what people believe is possible in robotics.

We love to see the things you’ve worked on. Have a portfolio or insane project you’ve worked on? Share it. We’re looking for people who push past the status quo, are passionate at work and in their own time—we’re looking for people who want to win.

notes: Robotics startup with strong engineering focus
